_provenance: V3.0 (2026-05-14): full voice-brief rewrite of all hint-state lines. Every line reauthored to Asia voice spec (domestic-administrative register, two-half sentence shape, attribution, no editorializing, no legal opinions, reception-desk work as default). Original V1.A provenance, scope, and engine-flag documentation preserved for reference. State IDs, triggers, tags, and on_dismiss blocks unchanged from V2 for engine compatibility. Coffee reactions preserve V1.4 gate logic with voice-corrected lines.
_scope: asia_hint_states_ch1.json owns Asia's repeatable hint-NPC surface for chapter 1. Encodes the get_asia_hint() first-unmet-flag-wins priority logic from story.txt lines 1245-1288. 16 states in priority order (12 progression + 4 coffee-result); the engine evaluates triggers top-to-bottom and returns the first matching state's line. All states are repeatable (no on_dismiss except hint_halina_arrived_announcement). Asia is the sole speaker; there is no turn structure.
_engine_flags_required: The engine calls get_asia_hint() when the player interacts with Asia's reception desk. Evaluate state triggers top-to-bottom; return the first match. All flags are in the chapter1 namespace. The default state (state 16) has no flag condition and always matches if no earlier state fires.
_address_forms: Asia does not address the player as Dr. A. Cula in hint-state lines (hint lines are mid-task, not greetings). Other NPCs: Mr. Pig, Mr. Murrow, Mrs. Sikorska, Mr. Swine (formal-titled); Crab, Whimsy (bare).
_comment_coffee_window_narrowing: Per user instruction, hint_coffee_skipped is gated on chapter1.coffee_tutorial_seen == false. That bare gate would match from game start; narrowing to (court_ready && !entered_court) confines all four coffee hints to the post-readiness, pre-court window where Asia would plausibly comment on the coffee choice.

states._comment: State 1 — player has not yet seen Pig's Beat-2 panic line; point toward Mr. Pig. Functional redirect, not narration.
lines: Mr. Pig is at his desk. He's been at his desk since seven. The kettle's been on since seven-fifteen, which means he's thinking about money again.

states._comment: State 2 — Pig crisis revealed; Murrow not yet met; point toward Mr. Murrow. Attribution: she names Murrow as the source, does not summarise the case.
lines: Mr. Murrow is in the archive. He'll have what you need. Mr. Pig has what you've already heard, which is mostly the sound of the envelope.

states._comment: State 3 — Murrow met; binder not yet collected; point toward archive and binder. Domestic translation: the binder as physical object. Four-step hint escalation pattern.
lines: The procedural binder. Blue spine, second shelf. If it's not on the second shelf, check the floor. If it's on the floor, it's been there since Tuesday.

states._comment: State 4 — binder collected; Crab not yet recruited; point toward Crab. She knows where everyone is. She does not editorialize on why Crab would help.
lines: Crab is at his desk. He's been reading something about service — I saw the page. He'll know if you ask him.

states._comment: State 5 — Crab recruited; Rights Memo not yet collected; point toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf. Domestic swerve: the memo's location identified by the noise around it.
lines: The rights memo. Whimsy may have it — try Café Paragraf. He's the one at the loudest table.

states._comment: State 6 — Rights Memo collected; Whimsy not yet recruited. Asia redirects without editorializing on Whimsy's personality. She has known him longer than anyone.
lines: Whimsy. He'll want to see the memo before he agrees to anything. That's just how he is — he needs the paper first and the flourish after.

states._comment: Beat 7 close announcement. Fires when Cula returns to the office after recruiting Whimsy. On dismiss, sets halina_arrived=true which gates Halina's NPC presence. Functional reception-desk line: she is announcing a visitor, not narrating a plot beat.
lines: Mrs. Sikorska is here. I've put her in the meeting room. She brought her own folder — it's organised.

states._comment: State 7 — Halina has arrived but the meeting hasn't happened. Repeatable redirect to meeting room. Domestic detail: the folder. No editorializing on the client.
lines: Mrs. Sikorska is still in the meeting room. She hasn't asked for anything — not even tea. She's patient.

states._comment: State 8 — client meeting concluded; Murrow in archive for Beat 9 research. Attribution: she names Murrow and describes what she can hear. The 'disappointed-page-turn sound' is hers — domestic diagnosis of archival activity.
lines: Mr. Murrow is in the archive with the binder. He's been turning pages — the slow kind of turning, where each page has let him down personally.

states._comment: State 9 — archive research complete; readiness check not yet completed. Points toward Murrow's checklist. Small competence: she knows his face for missing documents.
lines: Mr. Murrow wants to go through the file one more time before court. He has a face he makes when something is missing. I've seen the face.

states._comment: Coffee-result V1.4 — S-grade reaction. Post-readiness, pre-court window. Asia notices the machine behaved. Domestic register: the machine as a thing she deals with, not a character moment.
lines: That machine made a sound I haven't heard before. It sounded almost cooperative. I don't trust it.

states._comment: Coffee-result V1.4 — A/B-grade reaction. Two-half sentence: factual observation, then the swerve. No legal opinion on readiness.
lines: You look like someone who has had coffee. Whether that's the same as being prepared, I couldn't say.

states._comment: Coffee-result V1.4 — D/F-grade reaction. The observation is physical, not judgmental. Domestic translation: vibrating near a specific office object.
lines: You're standing very still and also somehow vibrating. I'm going to put a coaster under your cup.

states._comment: Coffee-result V1.4 — skipped reaction. Asia is not giving an opinion on the strategy. She is noting a fact from the desk.
lines: No coffee. Mr. Murrow went without coffee once and filed a motion in the wrong courtroom. I'm not saying there's a connection.

states._comment: State 10 — readiness check passed; court not yet resolved. Points north to District Court. chapter1.won_court is now declared in state.gd::reset_state().
lines: Mr. Murrow says you're ready. District Court is north. The courtroom is number four — I wrote it on a sticky note, it's on your folder.

states._comment: State 11 — court won; Beat-14 Swine postcard not yet arrived. Asia handles the post. She does not editorialize on Mr. Swine's absence. chapter1.won_court is now declared in state.gd::reset_state().
lines: Something came in the post from Mr. Swine. The stamp is from — I want to say Sapporo. The handwriting is his.

states._comment: State 12 — default; all chapter-1 flags satisfied. Post-chapter coda. Asia at her desk. The line does reception work: she is assessing the current state of the office as a workspace, not narrating the firm's survival.
lines: The phone has been quiet for eleven minutes. That's either a good sign or the line is dead. I'm choosing not to check.
